title: Complete clia-org's public-launch checklist (Pages site live, README polished, App Store presence)
description:
Successor bead to legacy incident 2026-02-04-clia-org-init (S1 CODE RED: CLIA-ORG INIT — 'let the world know about CLIA'). Reclassified 2026-05-26 from incident shape to bead because the original work isn't a reactive crisis with stop-world authority — it's the org's product launch, which now has a proper typed home: clia-org's founding mission statement at clia-org/private/universal/identity/clia-org@rismay.substrate.mission.json (typed OrgMissionStatementModel; declares CLIA bundle factory ikigai with digikoma/agent/ghost product axes).

The mission statement IS the launch's foundational typed record. This bead tracks the remaining launch CHECKLIST items — concrete burn-down work to make clia-org publicly visible:

1. CLIA-ORG Pages site live + reachable: verify https://clia-org.github.io/clia-org/ (or canonical URL) loads + DocC `/documentation/clia-org` landing page renders. Pages workflow at clia-org/public/gh-pages/clia-org.github.io/.github/workflows/publish.yml should be green on main.
2. Public README polished: clia-org/public/readmes/public-readme should explain CLIA = Command Line Interface Assistant, the .digikoma/.agent/.ghost taxonomy, and the substrate's CLIA bundle factory product line in operator-facing terms.
3. App Store presence: confirm clia-org's Apple apps (clia-agents, clia-app, clia-cli, etc.) at clia-org/private/apple/apps/ have App Store-distributable surfaces. Identify which is the canonical 'Operator app' the legacy incident referenced.
4. README + DocC cross-references: substrate-wide search for 'CLIA' should surface the typed mission statement + the .clia bundle architecture. Update Notion / public-facing docs to match.
5. Public messaging: when sites + apps are live, schedule social/blog publication. (Operator decision on cadence.)

NOT IN SCOPE for this bead:
- The .clia bundle implementation arc (separate bead: clia-bundle-suffix-grammar-substrate-adoption.issue.json — that's the typed-record + tooling work)
- Other substrate orgs' founding missions (separate bead: substrate-org-mission-statements-founding-sweep.issue.json)
- Any actual launch event marketing / amplification (operator-driven, not agent-burndown)

This bead is burn-down: each numbered item gets ticked off, the bead closes when all 5 are done or the operator declines remaining items. Closure transitions status:open → status:closed with resolutionNote citing what landed.
